 {"id":"bd-21uy","title":"Panel markup parsing tests","description":"## Problem
Panel titles and content may have similar markup parsing issues as Table cells. Need explicit tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Panel::new() with markup in title - verify markup is parsed
- [ ] Test Panel with markup in content - verify markup is parsed  
- [ ] Test Panel with nested markup styles
- [ ] Verify ANSI codes present in rendered output
- [ ] Verify raw markup tags NOT present in rendered output

## Implementation Notes
- Check if Panel already parses markup correctly
- If not, may need to add Panel::from_markup() similar to Cell
- Add tests either way to document and lock down behavior

## Files to modify
- tests/conformance/panel_tests.rs
- src/renderables/panel.rs (if fix needed)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:50.977751990Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:57:11.036711554Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T07:57:11.036694292Z","close_reason":"Created tests/conformance/panel_tests.rs with 14 tests covering: Panel::from_text() does not parse markup, title/subtitle do not parse markup, pre-styled Text preserves styles via from_rich_text(), nested styles work correctly, ANSI codes present in styled output, raw markup tags absent in pre-styled content. Documents that Panel behavior is consistent with Cell (markup not auto-parsed).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}

## Files to modify
- tests/conformance/progress_tests.rs
- src/renderables/progress.rs (if fix needed)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:54.496888342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T15:22:18.842739933Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T15:22:18.842679088Z","close_reason":"Progress markup conformance tests already present","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}

 {"id":"bd-2llx","title":"Regression test: Cell::new() vs Cell::from_markup()","description":"## Problem
Cell::new() was silently NOT parsing markup - users expected Cell::new("[bold]text[/]") to work but it didn't. We added Cell::from_markup() but need regression tests to prevent this from happening again and document the difference clearly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test that explicitly shows Cell::new("[bold]text[/]") does NOT parse markup (expected behavior, documents it)
- [ ] Test that explicitly shows Cell::from_markup("[bold]text[/]") DOES parse markup
- [ ] Test the difference in output between the two methods
- [ ] Add doc comments explaining when to use each method

## Implementation Notes
- The tests should be clear about the EXPECTED difference between the two methods
- This documents API behavior and prevents confusion

## Files to modify
- src/renderables/table.rs - improve doc comments
- tests/conformance/table_tests.rs - add regression tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:27.981429151Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:25:04.610863384Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:25:04.610835742Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added 6 regression tests documenting Cell::new() vs Cell::from_markup() difference. Updated Cell::new() doc comments with clear examples. All tests pass, clippy and fmt clean.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-2nv","title":"Targeted code review pass + file findings","description":"Perform focused code review for correctness/perf risks; file follow-up beads for any findings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T16:59:53.973055499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:21:57.601872493Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:21:57.601543252Z","close_reason":"Completed: filed findings bd-yb9 + bd-1wk","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2rqt","title":"API misuse detection tests","description":"## Problem
Users can easily misuse the API (e.g., using Cell::new() instead of Cell::from_markup()). We need tests that document these gotchas and ensure the API behaves predictably.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Document all methods that accept strings but DON'T parse markup
- [ ] Document all methods that DO parse markup  
- [ ] Add tests that explicitly show the difference
- [ ] Consider adding deprecation warnings or lints for confusing APIs

## API Audit checklist
- [ ] Cell::new() - does NOT parse markup (documented)
- [ ] Cell::from_markup() - DOES parse markup (documented)
- [ ] Text::new() - does NOT parse markup
- [ ] Text::from_markup() - DOES parse markup
- [ ] Panel title/content - audit and document
- [ ] Tree labels - audit and document
- [ ] Progress descriptions - audit and document

## Files to modify
- Add doc comments to all relevant structs/methods
- tests/conformance/*.rs - add explicit documentation tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T05:00:09.661046714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T10:00:40.238099951Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T10:00:40.238082187Z","close_reason":"Documented markup parsing behavior across Text/Panel/Tree/Progress/Table; added conformance tests for Text vs markup","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}

 {"id":"bd-3gpn","title":"Fresh-eyes audit pass: terminal detection + markup parser","description":"BV shows no open beads; creating a focused audit task. Add regression tests where needed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T15:22:22.322303363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T15:30:20.942891466Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T15:30:20.942871398Z","close_reason":"Fix: force_terminal now recomputes detected color system when no explicit color_system set","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3svf","title":"Full rendering pipeline integration tests","description":"## Problem
We need integration tests that verify the ENTIRE rendering pipeline from user API call to final output string. This would have caught the Cell markup bug.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Console.print() with various renderables containing markup
- [ ] Capture actual output string and verify markup is processed
- [ ] Test Table -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test Panel -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test nested renderables (Table in Panel, etc.) with markup

## Implementation Notes
- Use StringBuffer to capture output
- Check that no raw markup tags like [bold] remain in output
- Verify ANSI escape codes are present for styled content
- These are TRUE integration tests - testing the full stack

## Files to modify
- Create new tests/integration/*.rs if needed
- Or add to tests/conformance/ with clear naming","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T05:00:07.703397468Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T06:21:54.166416332Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T06:21:54.166109694Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added 5 full-pipeline integration tests to e2e_rendering.rs. Tests cover Table/Panel with markup, Cell::new vs from_markup differences, Console.print with markup, and nested markup styles. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-4zjp","title":"Audit existing tests for trivial assertions","description":"## Problem
We found trivial assertions like assert!(x.is_empty() || true) and assert!(!output.is_empty() || output.is_empty()) that were always true and provided no value. There may be more lurking in the codebase.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Audit all test files for trivial assertions that are always true/false
- [ ] Audit for assertions that don't actually verify behavior (just check non-empty)
- [ ] Replace trivial assertions with meaningful ones
- [ ] Add proper style/content verification where output is captured

## Patterns to find
- assert!(x || true)
- assert!(x || !x) 
- assert!(!x.is_empty()) without checking content
- let _ = something; // Just checking it doesn't crash

## Files to audit
- tests/conformance/*.rs
- src/**/*.rs (inline tests)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:49.161430662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:11:36.867626646Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:11:36.867605776Z","close_reason":"Replaced trivial assertions with meaningful content/width checks","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
-{"id":"bd-eb17","title":"Port rich.prompt/status/pager equivalents (interactive helpers)","description":"Implement the interactive helper layer from Python Rich.

Scope (Rust-idiomatic):
- Prompt: styled input with validation and defaults
- Status: spinner + message context manager
- Pager: send output through pager when available

Acceptance criteria:
- Cross-platform behavior (Unix + Windows) behind crossterm
- Unit tests for parsing/validation logic; integration tests for non-interactive fallback

 {"id":"bd-i1kt","title":"Port rich.emoji: emoji codes + Emoji renderable","description":"Implement Python Rich emoji support.

- Emoji code replacement in strings: :smile:, :smile-emoji:, :smile-text: (with variant selectors) when Console emoji is enabled.
- Provide an Emoji renderable/type similar to Python rich.emoji.Emoji.
- Use the same emoji name->unicode mapping as Python Rich (Rich 13.9.4 rich/_emoji_codes.py).

Acceptance criteria:
- Unit tests for replacement + variants + unknown code passthrough
- Console respects ConsoleBuilder::emoji(false)
- Conformance fixtures include at least one emoji-code case comparing against Python Rich
- Update docs (RICH_SPEC/FEATURE_PARITY/README if needed)
